motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 8°F at first light, climbing no higher than 14°F by afternoon. Pewter sky, low and close. The lake fully sealed, shore to shore. Dock flags down at noon. The hum returned at the ice-line at dusk, steady for eleven minutes and fourteen seconds.

undercurrent.note: CRESTING, fully in the open. A second letter to T. Aldercott has arrived — the channel has sent twice to the same name and no one has come yet. Calloway is present and has named what she is (a librarian, someone who indexes things) without naming what she found. The town is no longer wondering whether the box is strange; it is adjusting its procedure to accommodate a strange box, which is the town's defining move. Agnes's line 'the channel isn't particular' is the season's thesis stated plainly. The resolution begins when T. Aldercott either arrives or doesn't, and the town makes room either way. The undercurrent holds high because the second letter is unresolved. The rowboat has shifted north — it is moving. The flags came down without an order: the town's unconscious is ahead of its conscious. Wren's final sentence is the season's emotional peak in print.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
